title.en: Asuka-Bench: Benchmarking Code Agents on Underspecified User Intent and Multi-Round Refinement
title.zh: Asuka-Bench：对代码智能体在未明确用户意图与多轮精化任务上的基准测试

abstract: Existing code-generation benchmarks score a single mapping from a complete prompt to a one-shot output. However, real web development is different. Users seldom write a full spec at the start; many requirements only become clear once they look at an intermediate result and react to it. We present Asuka-Bench, a benchmark that pairs underspecified user intent with multi-round refinement, grounded in browser-rendered behavior. Each task is resolved through a closed loop: a Code Agent generates a web project, a UI Agent executes test cases on the deployed site, and a User LLM turns evaluation outcomes into natural-language feedback for the next round. The benchmark comprises 50 web tasks with 784 evaluation criteria and 2402 expected outcomes. We benchmark 8 LLMs across 2 agent frameworks. The results separate models clearly: weighted Task Pass Rate varies by 38 percentage points and models also differ substantially in their ability to repair from feedback. Asuka-Bench is also far from saturated: even the strongest model completes only 52% of projects after three rounds.

relevance.en: This paper extends evaluation methodology beyond single-shot pass@k by introducing multi-round refinement from underspecified intent, directly relevant to your interest in post-training with execution feedback. However, it focuses on web development tasks rather than repository-level code synthesis, so the connection to long-context handling is tangential.
relevance.zh: 本文通过引入从未明确意图出发的多轮精化，将评估方法扩展到了单次pass@k之外，这与您对执行反馈后训练的兴趣直接相关。然而，本文关注的是web开发任务而非仓库级代码合成，因此与长上下文处理的联系是间接的。

caption.en:
Comparison between existing paradigm and
Asuka-Bench. Existing paradigm (top) uses clarified
specs and single-turn execution; Asuka-Bench (bottom)
supports iterative refinement from underspecified intents
via agent feedback loops.
caption.zh: 现有范式与Asuka-Bench的对比。现有范式（上图）使用明确的规范与单轮执行；Asuka-Bench（下图）通过智能体反馈循环支持从未明确意图出发的迭代精化。

caption.en:
Overview of Asuka-Bench. Top: Dataset construction—raw queries from three sources are rewritten into
a Clarified PRD, from which an underspecified query, simulated data, and DAG-organized evaluation criteria are
derived. Bottom-left: Evaluation loop—a Code Agent generates a web project from the underspecified query; a UI
Agent executes criteria as browser-based test cases; a User LLM synthesizes pass/fail results into feedback for the
next refinement round.
caption.zh: Asuka-Bench概览。顶部：数据集构建——来自三个来源的原始查询被重写为澄清的产品需求文档，从中衍生出未明确的查询、模拟数据和以有向无环图组织的评估标准。左下：评估循环——代码智能体从未明确查询生成web项目；UI智能体将标准作为基于浏览器的测试用例执行；用户大语言模型将通过/失败结果综合成反馈以进行下一轮精化。

caption.en:
The web-based annotation console used by human annotators. The deployed project under evaluation is
rendered live in the centre pane; the right pane lists the per-criterion checklist grouped by task; annotators interact
with the live application and judge each criterion as Pass or Fail before saving.
caption.zh: 人工标注者使用的基于web的标注控制台。被评估的已部署项目在中央窗格中实时渲染；右窗格列出按任务分组的标准检查清单；标注者与实时应用程序交互，并将每个标准判断为通过或失败，然后保存。

figures.reference_text: This appendix records the rubric and conventions that human annotators followed to produce the gold labels used in RQ3, together with the an- notation console (Figure 7) used to apply them. Annotation is single-annotated (one independent annotator per criterion) and applied only to the final-round (Round 3) deployed application; in- termediate rounds are not annotated. Each cri- terion is judged independently and the resulting JSON file follows the exact same schema as our au- tomated evaluator’s eval_step3_results.json, which makes the agreement comparison schema- aligned by construction.

caption.en:
Overview of Asuka-Bench. Top: Dataset construction—raw queries from three sources are rewritten into
a Clarified PRD, from which an underspecified query, simulated data, and DAG-organized evaluation criteria are
derived. Bottom-left: Evaluation loop—a Code Agent generates a web project from the underspecified query; a UI
Agent executes criteria as browser-based test cases; a User LLM synthesizes pass/fail results into feedback for the
next refinement round.
caption.zh: Asuka-Bench概览。顶部：数据集构建——来自三个来源的原始查询被重写为澄清的产品需求文档，从中衍生出未明确的查询、模拟数据和以有向无环图组织的评估标准。左下：评估循环——代码智能体从未明确查询生成web项目；UI智能体将标准作为基于浏览器的测试用例执行；用户大语言模型将通过/失败结果综合成反馈以进行下一轮精化。

brief_summary.en:
Existing code-generation benchmarks like HumanEval score a single mapping from a complete prompt to a one-shot output, but real web development rarely works that way—users start with vague ideas and refine them after seeing a prototype. This gap means current evaluations cannot measure a code agent’s ability to handle underspecified intent or to repair code from iterative feedback. Asuka-Bench directly addresses this by pairing deliberately ambiguous user queries with a multi-round refinement loop: a Code Agent generates a web project, a UI Agent runs browser-based tests, and a User LLM turns pass/fail outcomes into natural-language feedback for the next round.

The benchmark comprises 50 web tasks with 784 evaluation criteria and 2,402 expected outcomes, organized as a directed acyclic graph to handle dependencies. Testing eight LLMs across two agent frameworks reveals a 38-percentage-point spread in weighted task pass rate, and even the strongest model completes only 52% of projects after three rounds—showing the benchmark is far from saturated. Crucially, models differ substantially in their ability to repair from feedback, a capability that single-shot benchmarks cannot capture, making Asuka-Bench a more realistic signal for post-training methods like reinforcement learning with execution feedback.
brief_summary.zh:
现有的代码生成基准（如HumanEval）只评估从完整提示到一次性输出的单一映射，但真实的web开发很少如此——用户通常从模糊的想法开始，在看到原型后逐步细化。这一差距意味着当前的评估无法衡量代码智能体处理未明确意图的能力，也无法衡量其根据迭代反馈修复代码的能力。Asuka-Bench直接弥补了这一不足，它将刻意模糊的用户查询与多轮精化循环相结合：代码智能体生成web项目，UI智能体执行基于浏览器的测试，用户大语言模型将测试的通过/失败结果转化为自然语言反馈，供下一轮使用。

该基准包含50个web任务，共计784个评估标准和2402个预期结果，并采用有向无环图组织以处理依赖关系。在两种智能体框架上测试八种大语言模型的结果显示，加权任务通过率存在38个百分点的差异，即使是最强的模型在三轮后也仅完成52%的项目——这表明该基准远未饱和。关键的是，不同模型在根据反馈进行修复的能力上存在显著差异，这是单次基准无法捕捉的，这使得Asuka-Bench成为强化学习（结合执行反馈）等训练后方法更接近现实的信号。

problem.en: Existing code-generation benchmarks, such as HumanEval, SWE-Bench, and their derivatives, evaluate a single mapping from a complete, well-specified prompt to a one-shot output. This paradigm fundamentally mismatches real-world web development, where users rarely provide a full specification upfront. Instead, requirements emerge iteratively as users inspect intermediate results and react to them. Prior benchmarks fail to capture this iterative, feedback-driven refinement process, meaning they cannot measure a code agent's ability to handle underspecified intent—where the initial query is ambiguous and incomplete—or its capability to repair and improve code across multiple rounds based on natural-language feedback. This gap is practically significant because developing robust code agents that can collaborate with users in realistic, dynamic workflows requires evaluation of exactly these abilities: interpreting vague requests, generating a reasonable first attempt, and then effectively incorporating feedback to fix bugs and add features. Without a benchmark that systematically tests multi-round refinement from underspecified intents, progress in building truly interactive coding assistants cannot be properly measured.
problem.zh: 现有的代码生成基准，如HumanEval、SWE-Bench及其衍生版本，评估的是从完整、良好指定的提示到一次性输出的单一映射。这种范式与真实的web开发根本不符，因为用户很少一开始就提供完整的规范。相反，需求是在用户检查中间结果并做出反应时迭代产生的。先前的基准未能捕捉这种迭代的、反馈驱动的精化过程，这意味着它们无法衡量代码智能体处理未明确意图的能力——即初始查询是模糊且不完整的——也无法衡量其根据自然语言反馈在多轮中修复和改进代码的能力。这一差距具有实际意义，因为开发能够在现实、动态的工作流程中与用户协作的稳健代码智能体，需要准确评估这些能力：解释模糊请求、生成合理的初次尝试，然后有效地整合反馈来修复错误和添加功能。如果没有一个系统性地测试从未明确意图出发的多轮精化的基准，构建真正交互式编码助手的进展就无法得到恰当的衡量。

solution.en: Asuka-Bench introduces a new benchmark paradigm that directly addresses the gap between single-shot benchmarks and real-world iterative development. Its core contribution is a closed-loop evaluation framework that pairs underspecified user intent with multi-round refinement, grounded in browser-rendered, behavioral test outcomes. Instead of a static prompt-to-output scoring, each task is resolved through an iterative loop: a Code Agent generates a complete web project from a deliberately vague initial query; a UI Agent then deploys the project and executes test cases by performing browser actions to verify functional behavior; the results are fed to a User LLM that synthesizes pass/fail outcomes into natural-language feedback for the next round. The key insight is that this loop—underspecified query → code generation → automated browser testing → feedback synthesis → regeneration—mirrors how actual users work: they start with an incomplete idea, see a prototype, and refine. The benchmark judges both the final quality after a fixed number of rounds (three by default) and the trajectory of improvement across rounds, explicitly separating a model's baseline generation ability from its repair-from-feedback ability.
solution.zh: Asuka-Bench引入了一种新的基准范式，直接弥合了单次基准与真实迭代开发之间的差距。其核心贡献是一个闭环评估框架，将未明确的用户意图与多轮精化配对，并基于浏览器渲染的行为测试结果。每个任务不是通过静态的从提示到输出的评分来解决，而是通过一个迭代循环来解决：代码智能体从一个故意模糊的初始查询生成一个完整的web项目；然后UI智能体部署该项目，并通过执行浏览器操作来验证功能行为，从而执行测试用例；结果被输送给用户大语言模型，该模型将通过/失败结果综合成下一轮的自然语言反馈。关键见解在于，这个循环——未明确查询 → 代码生成 → 自动化浏览器测试 → 反馈合成 → 重新生成——镜像了实际用户的工作方式：他们从一个不完整的想法开始，看到原型，然后细化。该基准既评估固定轮数（默认为三轮）后的最终质量，也评估跨轮次的改进轨迹，明确区分了模型的基础生成能力与从反馈中修复的能力。

methodology.en: The Asuka-Bench methodology encompasses two major components: dataset construction and the evaluation loop. **Dataset Construction:** 50 web development tasks are sourced from three origins: existing code benchmarks (ArtifactsBench, WebArena, etc.), open-source web apps, and manual design. Each raw query is first rewritten by a 'Query Clarifier LLM' (DeepSeek-R1 with three human-verified few-shot examples) into a Clarified Product Requirements Document (Clarified PRD) that specifies all functional details. From this Clarified PRD, three parallel artifacts are derived: (1) an **Underspecified Query** — a shortened, ambiguous version that omits details; (2) **Simulated Data** — dummy content (e.g., example users, products) to populate the app; (3) a **DAG-organized set of Evaluation Criteria** — 784 criteria in total (averaging ~16 per task), organized as a directed acyclic graph where parent criteria must pass before children are evaluated, with 2,402 expected outcomes (pass/fail). Six application categories are covered: Utility (38%), Data Visualization (22%), Management System (20%), E-Commerce (8%), Marketing/Display (6%), and Social (6%). **Evaluation Loop:** The loop runs for up to 3 rounds by default (or more for analysis). In each round: (a) a **Code Agent** takes the Underspecified Query (Round 1) or the natural-language feedback from the previous round (Rounds 2+) and generates a complete web project (HTML/CSS/JS or similar files); (b) a **UI Agent** (browser automation tool) deploys the project, then for each criterion not yet passed and whose parent criteria are satisfied, it performs a browser-based test case (e.g., click button, check DOM content, verify text appears), producing a pass/fail result with a screenshot; (c) a **User LLM** (a separate model selected from GPT-4o, Gemini-2.5-Flash, or LLaMA-3.1-70B, tuned per experiment) takes the set of pass/fail results from the current round along with the screenshots and generates a natural-language feedback message describing what needs to be fixed, mirroring how a human user would react. The feedback, plus the agent's own chat history, is fed to the Code Agent for the next round. Two agent frameworks are used: **Claude Code** (Anthropic's official agent) and **OpenHands** (an open-source agent framework). Eight different LLMs are tested as the backbone of the Code Agent. Key design decisions include: using the DAG to avoid evaluating subtasks whose prerequisites are not met; using a separate User LLM to ensure reproducible, non-human feedback; and capping rounds to control evaluation cost.
methodology.zh: Asuka-Bench方法论包含两个主要部分：数据集构建和评估循环。**数据集构建：** 50个web开发任务来自三个来源：现有代码基准（ArtifactsBench、WebArena等）、开源web应用和手动设计。每个原始查询首先由一个‘查询澄清大语言模型’（使用三个经过人工验证的少样本示例的DeepSeek-R1）重写为一个澄清的产品需求文档，该文档指定了所有功能细节。从这个澄清的产品需求文档中，衍生出三个并行的人工制品：（1）**未明确的查询**——一个缩短的、模糊的版本，省略了细节；（2）**模拟数据**——用于填充应用程序的虚拟内容（例如，示例用户、产品）；（3）**一组以有向无环图组织的评估标准**——总共784个标准（平均每任务约16个），组织成一个有向无环图，其中父标准必须通过后才能评估子标准，共有2402个预期结果（通过/失败）。涵盖六类应用：实用工具（38%）、数据可视化（22%）、管理系统（20%）、电子商务（8%）、营销/展示（6%）和社交（6%）。**评估循环：** 默认情况下循环最多运行3轮（或更多用于分析）。在每一轮中：（a）**代码智能体**接收未明确的查询（第1轮）或上一轮的自然语言反馈（第2轮及以后），并生成一个完整的web项目（HTML/CSS/JS或类似文件）；（b）**UI智能体**（浏览器自动化工具）部署该项目，然后对于每个尚未通过且其父标准满足的标准，它执行一个基于浏览器的测试用例（例如，点击按钮，检查DOM内容，验证文本出现），产生一个通过/失败结果和截图；（c）**用户大语言模型**（一个独立的模型，选自GPT-4o、Gemini-2.5-Flash或LLaMA-3.1-70B，根据实验调整）接收当前轮次的一组通过/失败结果以及截图，并生成一个自然语言反馈消息，描述需要修复的内容，模拟人类用户的反应方式。反馈以及智能体自己的聊天历史被输入给代码智能体用于下一轮。使用了两个智能体框架：**Claude Code**（Anthropic的官方智能体）和**OpenHands**（一个开源智能体框架）。八种不同的大语言模型作为代码智能体的主干进行测试。关键设计决策包括：使用有向无环图避免评估前提条件未满足的子任务；使用独立的用户大语言模型以确保可重复的、非人工的反馈；以及限制轮数以控制评估成本。

experiments.en: The benchmark evaluates 8 large language models across 2 agent frameworks (Claude Code and OpenHands) using the default 3-round setting. **Metrics:** The primary metric is Weighted Task Pass Rate (WTPR), which weights each criterion by its depth in the DAG to account for dependency complexity, and also reports Cumulative Project Completion Rate (CPCR, the percentage of tasks where all criteria pass). **Key Results:** (1) Models are clearly separated—WTPR varies by 38 percentage points across models (from roughly 30% to 68% on Claude Code). (2) Even the strongest model (Claude-4.6-Sonnet) completes only 52% of projects after 3 rounds, indicating the benchmark is far from saturated. (3) **Repair-from-feedback ability** is shown to be a separable dimension: comparing a model's performance on a single round with the Clarified PRD (full spec) versus three rounds on the underspecified query reveals that the first ambiguous round loses 9–18 percentage points relative to the PRD baseline, but stronger models close this gap by Round 3, while weaker models retain a persistent deficit. (4) A deeper analysis of the gap between 'Implemented' tasks (tasks the model attempted, i.e., those reachable under the DAG) and 'Passed' tasks shows that stronger models close this gap quickly (GPT-5.4 on OpenHands reaches 99.5% implemented vs 90.1% passed at Round 3), while weaker models retain a persistent gap. (5) Human annotation of 5 tasks for 4 models shows 75–95% agreement with the automated UI Agent, validating the automated evaluation. (6) Extended experiments to 8 rounds show that even the strongest model can plateau—Claude-4.6-Sonnet reaches 100% CPCR and WTPR only by Round 8, demonstrating the difficulty of full completion. (7) Ablation: swapping the User LLM between models shows that GPT-4o provides the most effective feedback, improving final WTPR by up to 11% over LLaMA-3.1-70B. **Datasets used:** 50 tasks from the Asuka-Bench dataset itself; no external benchmarks are used for comparison since this is a new benchmark. **Baselines:** All 8 LLMs serve as baselines against each other; no prior benchmarks are compared because the paradigm is novel.
experiments.zh: 该基准在默认的三轮设置下，在2个智能体框架（Claude Code和OpenHands）上评估了8种大语言模型。**指标：** 主要指标是加权任务通过率，它根据每个标准在有向无环图中的深度进行加权以考虑依赖复杂性，同时还报告累积项目完成率（所有标准都通过的任务百分比）。**关键结果：** (1) 模型被清晰地区分开——加权任务通过率在模型间差异达38个百分点（在Claude Code上从大约30%到68%）。(2) 即使是最强的模型（Claude-4.6-Sonnet）在三轮后也仅完成52%的项目，表明该基准远未饱和。(3) **从反馈中修复的能力**被证明是一个可分离的维度：比较模型在单轮中使用澄清的产品需求文档（完整规范）的表现与在未明确查询上使用三轮的表现，发现第一轮模糊查询相对于澄清的产品需求文档基线损失了9-18个百分点，但更强的模型通过第三轮缩小了差距，而较弱的模型则持续存在差距。(4) 对‘已实现’任务（模型尝试过的任务，即在有向无环图中可达的任务）和‘已通过’任务之间差距的深入分析表明，更强的模型快速缩小了这一差距（GPT-5.4在OpenHands上第三轮达到了99.5%已实现 vs 90.1%已通过），而较弱的模型则保留了持续差距。(5) 对4个模型的5个任务进行的人工标注显示与自动化UI智能体有75-95%的一致性，验证了自动化评估。(6) 扩展到8轮的实验表明，即使是最强的模型也可能达到平台期——Claude-4.6-Sonnet直到第8轮才达到100%累积项目完成率和加权任务通过率，展示了完全完成的难度。(7) 消融实验：在模型之间交换用户大语言模型表明，GPT-4o提供最有效的反馈，最终加权任务通过率比LLaMA-3.1-70B提高高达11%。**使用的数据集：** 来自Asuka-Bench数据集本身的50个任务；没有使用外部基准进行比较，因为这是一个新基准。**基线：** 所有8种大语言模型互为基线；没有与以前的基准进行比较，因为这种范式是新颖的。

conclusion.en: Asuka-Bench reveals that multi-round refinement from underspecified user intent is a distinct and challenging capability—models strong at single-shot generation often fail to effectively repair code from iterative feedback, with a 38-point gap between the best and worst performers. Even the top model completes only 52% of projects after three rounds, indicating that no current code agent has mastered this interactive, realistic coding scenario. The benchmark's closed-loop setup, which mirrors real web development cycles, provides a more discriminating signal for post-training methods than single-shot benchmarks. However, the paper does not analyze specific failure modes or correlations between initial generation and repair ability, leaving open questions about which interactive capabilities are most needed. These findings imply that future research should prioritize building iterative repair and user-intent understanding, not just one-shot code generation.
conclusion.zh: Asuka-Bench揭示了从未明确用户意图出发的多轮精化是一个独特且具有挑战性的能力——擅长单次生成的模型通常无法有效地从迭代反馈中修复代码，最佳和最差表现者之间存在38个百分点的差距。即使是顶级模型在三轮后也仅完成52%的项目，表明目前没有代码智能体掌握这种交互式的、现实的编码场景。该基准的闭环设置反映了真实的web开发周期，为训练后方法提供了比单次基准更具区分度的信号。然而，论文没有分析具体的故障模式或初始生成能力与修复能力之间的相关性，留下了关于哪些交互能力最需要被优先考虑的问题。这些发现表明，未来的研究应优先构建迭代修复和用户意图理解，而不仅仅是一次性代码生成。

question.en: How does the multi-round refinement loop in Asuka-Bench provide a more realistic signal for post-training methods (e.g., RL with execution feedback) compared to single-shot pass@k on isolated functions, given that the benchmark measures a model's ability to repair code based on natural-language feedback from execution outcomes?
question.zh: 与孤立函数上的单次pass@k相比，Asuka-Bench中的多轮精化循环如何为训练后方法（例如，基于执行反馈的强化学习）提供更接近现实的信号？该基准衡量的是模型根据执行结果的自然语言反馈修复代码的能力。

reason.en: Directly addresses the user's interest in post-training techniques and execution feedback, moving beyond isolated-function metrics. The paper's closed-loop framework (Code Agent -> UI Agent -> User LLM -> next round) mirrors the iterative cycle that RL with execution feedback targets. Exploring how Asuka-Bench's repair capability metric (WTPR per round) could serve as a training or evaluation signal for RL methods is a deep, paper-grounded insight.
reason.zh: 直接涉及用户对训练后技术和执行反馈的兴趣，超越了孤立函数指标。论文的闭环框架（代码智能体 -> UI智能体 -> 用户大语言模型 -> 下一轮）反映了强化学习结合执行反馈所针对的迭代循环。探索Asuka-Bench的修复能力指标（每轮加权任务通过率）如何作为强化学习方法的训练或评估信号，是一个基于论文的深刻见解。

question.en: How does the multi-round refinement loop in Asuka-Bench provide a more realistic signal for post-training methods (e.g., RL with execution feedback) compared to single-shot pass@k on isolated functions, given that the benchmark measures a model's ability to repair code based on natural-language feedback from execution outcomes?
question.zh: 与孤立函数上的单次pass@k相比，Asuka-Bench中的多轮精化循环如何为训练后方法（例如，基于执行反馈的强化学习）提供更接近现实的信号？该基准衡量的是模型根据执行结果的自然语言反馈修复代码的能力。

reason.en: Directly addresses the user's interest in post-training techniques and execution feedback, moving beyond isolated-function metrics. The paper's closed-loop framework (Code Agent -> UI Agent -> User LLM -> next round) mirrors the iterative cycle that RL with execution feedback targets. Exploring how Asuka-Bench's repair capability metric (WTPR per round) could serve as a training or evaluation signal for RL methods is a deep, paper-grounded insight.
reason.zh: 直接涉及用户对训练后技术和执行反馈的兴趣，超越了孤立函数指标。论文的闭环框架（代码智能体 -> UI智能体 -> 用户大语言模型 -> 下一轮）反映了强化学习结合执行反馈所针对的迭代循环。探索Asuka-Bench的修复能力指标（每轮加权任务通过率）如何作为强化学习方法的训练或评估信号，是一个基于论文的深刻见解。

answer.en: The multi-round refinement loop in Asuka-Bench provides a more realistic signal for post-training methods like RL with execution feedback because it directly mirrors the iterative repair process those methods target, rather than testing isolated one-shot generation. The paper states that real web development is iterative: "Users seldom write a full spec at the start; many requirements only become clear once they look at an intermediate result and react to it." Accordingly, Asuka-Bench's closed loop—where a "User LLM turns evaluation outcomes into natural-language feedback for the next round"—exactly replicates the cycle an RL policy would experience: receive execution feedback, refine, and repeat. The benchmark measures repair capability per round (Weighted Task Pass Rate across rounds), which can serve as a dense training signal; the paper notes that "models also differ substantially in their ability to repair from feedback" and that "even the strongest model completes only 52% of projects after three rounds," showing that the signal is non-trivial and far from saturated. This contrasts with single-shot pass@k on isolated functions, which neither requires handling underspecified intent nor provides multi-turn feedback loops.
answer.zh: Asuka-Bench中的多轮精化循环为强化学习（结合执行反馈）等训练后方法提供了更接近现实的信号，因为它直接镜像了这些方法所针对的迭代修复过程，而不是测试孤立的单次生成。论文指出真实的web开发是迭代的：“用户很少在开始时写出完整的规范；许多需求只有在他们查看中间结果并做出反应时才变得清晰。”相应地，Asuka-Bench的闭环——其中“用户大语言模型将评估结果转化为下一轮的自然语言反馈”——精确地复制了强化学习策略会经历的循环：接收执行反馈，精化，重复。该基准衡量每轮的修复能力（各轮加权任务通过率），这可以作为密集的训练信号；论文指出“模型在根据反馈进行修复的能力上也存在显著差异”，并且“即使是最强的模型在三轮后也仅完成52%的项目”，表明该信号是重要的且远未饱和。这与孤立函数上的单次pass@k形成对比，后者既不需要处理未明确的意图，也不提供多轮反馈循环。
